asset_name: Summary overlay — Round results and pause frame
shared_properties: The overlay uses a large centered panel format suitable for end-of-round summaries and pause-state information.
Core palette fields remain fixed at primary #111111, background #000000, and accent #FFFFFF.
Monochrome blocks separate score, survival status, and action prompts without relying on hue.
differences: The panel includes a headline zone, metric grid, event recap gutter, and action-footer area for continue or retry prompts.
Metric cells are large enough to display population, buildings, queue accuracy, and winter outcome at a glance.
A dim outer veil frames the panel so the paused world remains visible but subdued in the background.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the winter survival loop, queue-memory mechanic, building roster, and 1920x1080 Bevy target.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the monochrome blizzard mood, Viking settlement silhouettes, and snow density targets.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Large centered overlay panel paired with a dim screen veil for pause, result, and end-of-winter transitions.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load ui/art_000019.png during UI boot and render it over a semi-opaque screen veil when pause or result states are active.
runtime_notes: Keep the overlay inside the center safe area so the top HUD and portrait can remain visible if needed.
Expose metric-cell anchors for runtime text injection instead of painting numbers into the texture.
Fade the veil independently from the panel so transitions can stage background visibility cleanly.

context.usage: Large summary panel used for winter results, prosperity checks, and pause-state status review.

shape.silhouette: Large centered results board with headline strip, metric grid, recap gutter, footer actions, and an outer dim veil.

acceptance_criteria: PNG canvas is exactly 840x560px with a clear metric grid and footer action zone.
Headline, metrics, recap, and footer remain visually distinct in grayscale only.
The overlay leaves enough negative space for dynamic text without crowding the panel borders.
